['Inconvenient service times', 'Physical location', 'Other barrier', 'As a person who pushed to work throughout my treatment, I was not able to participate in many amazing classes and services that Dempsey Center offered in Lewiston or Portland. I also fell through the cracks initially with my request to connect through one of the programs and did not get a response after filling out repeated forms with basic demographic info. column19: The Dempsey Center provides such important assistance to those in treatment & their families/care givers.  Treatment is not about feeling better, it's about halting cancer.  The DC provides us with a tool kit and support for the little imbalances and the greater upsets we have to navigate through.  It is simply the most important relationship in a cancer patient's life, we are so lucky to have this available to us in this area.

column19: It was a surprise for me to find out how valuable, in my life with this chronic disease, are the services that I continue to use through the Dempsey Center.  I meet with Kerry Irish, by zoom, for private counseling which has helped me feel more powerful, optimistic, and able to communicate with family and friends. Also, I was NOT going to join a support group, but on her recommendation to just try it, I did join, and after these months with our groups weekly zoom meetings, I feel the support they lend, the humor, suggestions, sharing of grief, and the support I can give back, has been remarkable. I am so grateful for Kerry's guidance and I share to others that The Dempsey Center offers so much that no one else has. I applaud the center.

column19: 98% supportive and client centered services.  I did have one very negative interaction on an intial visit with an acupuncturist.  The coordinator heard of it, called me and listened.  She summarized this interaction was against the primary Dempsey purpose of always respecting and taking their lead from the client.    I let some time go by as it was very upsetting but ultimatey benefited from another provider.    One suggestion:   Clarify when and if it is expected to notify someone when you are unable to attend a session of a virtual session you are registered to attend.  I've received differing answers.  It is amazing at a time of uncertainty,  the Center is able to provide acceptance, support and such a variety of services that  unbeknownst to   them brought me from a dark place to life.  The positive calm demeanor of volunteers and paid staff is amazing.   Thank you to all!    BYW I hope there are supportive services to all staff as I'm sure they deal with extremely difficult situations and emotional goodbyes.

column19: The Dempsey Center has been incredibly helpful to me during a difficult diagnosis. I have been taking multiple online fitness and meditation classes for almost a year, have had consults with Tish and Judy when surgery or other factors impacted my nutrition or fitness needs, and therapy with Christine is an INCREDIBLE gift--I adore her!    I'm glad support groups work for others, but when my care partner and I tried them, we found them stressful in the face of my own uncertain prognosis, because people were often sharing dire news and it was hard to hear and anxiety-producing. Because I am in "watch and wait" mode, I'm not sure if I'm a candidate for a Survivorship program or not, but even if I am, I'm a wary of joining one because if I hear a lot of stories of difficulty, it can be activating for me at a time when I'm trying to find calm. I'm not sure what to do about this, and it's obviously my own problem, but I'm thinking that maybe some one-on-one coaching for survivors that could be more tailored to individual needs would be amazing. For example, I am struggling with emotional issues--anxiety, fear, trauma--because of a sudden diagnosis at a young age and a situation that doesn't lend itself to an NED (because the spots seen on the scans are too small to be biopsied, but also don't seem to be growing or changing), but I'm not having any physical impacts from my treatment (which I know is very lucky!). So it would be tough to be in a Survivorship group with people who had more conclusive results OR were dealing with physical impacts I'm fortunate to not be experiencing.    All that said, I am SO grateful to the Dempsey and feel so lucky that we have this incredible resource here in this state. I really don't know what I would do without it.

column16: ['Long wait for orientation', 'Long wait for services', 'I went to the Dempsey Center as soon as I had had a very serious cancer diagnosis.  I was told on the phone about all the services; however, when I came as I was in immunotherapy, I was told there was nothing at all open at this time, but to keep checking in.  I asked why and they said they not only give services ie. therapy, and massage therapy to Cancer Patients but also to their family and care givers etc.   After going to the Dempsey Center over 4 times, the 4th being promised all the care I sought, I never got in.  After 1.5 years that record is pathetic.  I still wish I had people to talk to now, but I am paying for the few sessions I can afford.  I am so happy that my therapy at New England Cancer Center resolved and healed a very dangerous situation.  They team I had there was the best in the world as was my oncologist.  The Dempsey Center is a Fraud for Non-Profit Cancer help, and should be shut down.']
column18: ["I have a counselor I have because in over a year 3 to 4 meetings of promises, I never received any help that was promised.  I did get one acupuncture therapy which was on a lawn chair with 8 other people attending the same.  I was given two needles in the foot and hand which did nothing.  I have been to real Acupuncture in K'bunk and from a woman who was trained and licensed in her native Country of China.  The Dempsey Center Dope put me in a lawn chair far too short for my 6'1 height and seriously aggravated an old injury from a car accident I had not felt until the Malpractice from the Dempsey Center Acupuncturist.  The is no acupuncturist in China where it was perfected 2000 years ago nor any acupuncturist that I ever went to that does this very special practice on a lawn chair. When I was in pain, the Dempsey acupuncturist was nowhere to be found-not in the room."]

I look forward to continuing to be involved with Dempsey Center.

column19: The only thing that was kind of a problem for us is that I did not have and do not have a single specific caregiver. I would like to have been able to offer to split the caregiver benefits between two people but the center does not allow that. My sister and my best girlfriend were my two loyal caregivers. My former partner who did not rise to the occasion of helping me through this difficult situation was registered as my caregiver but then we broke up and he never used any of the services either. So in my situation, neither  of my  actual caregivers has  gotten any services. I feel kind of bad about that and i couldn’t chose between them…

column14: ['Reduced emotional distress and/or psychological burden (e.g., reduced anxiety, reduced depression, reduced feelings of sadness, reduced nervousness, appropriate processing of grief, etc.)', 'Reduced isolation; created community with others impacted by cancer', 'Helped me rebuild or strengthen my sense of self/identity', 'Probably the  greatest impact on my life, because of classes at the Dempsey Center and my on-going support group facilitated by Kerry Irish, is a greater sense of control  in my life and body after feeling as if I had lost all that kept me safe. Through the interaction with others with canter at the Demsey Center, and working privately with Kerry, as well as in our group, I have gained a new and stronger sense of myself, a determination to live as fully as I can, and to leave behind everything that does not serve me in this fabulous life. It  has helped me clarify how I want to live, how to be supportive of myself, along with supporting and caring for my family and friends. The wonderful support group I am in gives me a nonjudgmental place to be frightened or sad, as well as a place to laugh at the occasional, strangely, hilarious moments living with a chronic disease can bring. What the Dempsey Center has  given me is the message it is not all about cancer, it is about LIFE. Thank you to all of you and your dedication to offering us so much learning and support.']

column14: ['Reduced emotional distress and/or psychological burden (e.g., reduced anxiety, reduced depression, reduced feelings of sadness, reduced nervousness, appropriate processing of grief, etc.)', 'Reduced isolation; created community with others impacted by cancer', 'Improved interpersonal relationships (friends, family, coworkers, etc.)', 'As a care partner The Dempsey Center was invaluable to me. I was in a zoom group for advanced care partners. I made this group a big priority in my life. I felt I had, guidance, support, understanding, validation and mostly being heard. Group was invaluable for me. I was blessed with an amazing group of people and we all became close. When someone’s person passed I know we all felt extreme feelings for them, but we also knew we wouldn’t be seeing these people any longer. That part is difficult, for sure. It’s important for care partners to have guidance. Of course the cancer patients are most important however, care partners need help. Some much more than others. That is an area that should be acknowledged.']
column18: ['I already knew about the Dempsey Center, so I was fortunate. If I didn’t, I would have asked NECS if they had recommendations. If not, internet research. One for private therapist that specializes in cancer, grief and also groups for the same.']
column19: Without the Dempsey Center I do not know where I would be right now.   My unending, sincere, heartfelt appreciation and gratitude goes out to The Dempsey Center.   Warmly,  A grieving mother    I do wish I was a therapist. I would love to volunteer to work with little children. Cover some of the things I wrote about for children.   When I feel I am able I do plan to see if I could volunteer there in anyway.

column19: As soon as husband passed, household income was cut in half. Being under full retirement age disqualified me from collecting my husband’s full social security. It is devastating immediately following husband passing

column14: ['Reduced emotional distress and/or psychological burden (e.g., reduced anxiety, reduced depression, reduced feelings of sadness, reduced nervousness, appropriate processing of grief, etc.)', 'Improved interpersonal relationships (friends, family, coworkers, etc.)', 'Having the Dempsey as a resource and incredible one! Has helped me so much through this time with my son as he fights colorectal cancer.  I loved having reiki to help regulate my anxiety, and relax my nervous system. Massage to help my tense muscles and counseling with Erin has been incredibly helpful. I am so grateful for this service and support as a parent who felt so helpless and so scared of loosing my son. column16: ['Long wait for services', 'Inconvenient service times', 'Physical location', "I understand that I was trying to access services at a challenging time for the Dempsey Center in the months before and during their location move.  I needed support last summer and fall in the early months of my diagnosis, and the wait time for services was significant. I wasn't able to schedule massage, counseling, or acupuncture and finally gave up because of the wait time."]
column18: ['Any licensed place that does lymphatic massage and acupuncture - this is not easy to find in an area outside of Portland/Westbrook.']
column19: I have struggled with the lack of support from the Dempsey Center at a time when I needed it when I was first diagnosed and asked for counseling, lymphatic massage and/or acupuncture.  Nothing happened for follow up until I reached out again and was told I was on a waiting list. I'm not interested in the group classes or big rooms of sharing feelings. I did ask for individual support and understand it was a time when the center was looking for a new location. The mission of your center is really good, and so is the work you do. I know you help many people. I think I fell through the cracks, so if there is a pause going forward, maybe remember that there are others who don't live in the neighborhood and still need support. If you can expand your outreach, that would be wonderful. This survey is meant to be anonymous, but I own my words here and may reach out with a phone call at some point as well. Thank you.

column19: From the minute that I walked into the Dempsey Center for the very first time, I knew that this was a place where I could really show my fear. Everywhere else, people kept telling me how much cancer treatment treatments had improved , that I would be fine, and that I was healthy other than cancer. At the Dempsey Center, when I sat in the lounge and yeah tears ran down my checks, people  understood. No one tried to pat me on the back and give what they perceived as encouraging words; they just let me cry. I did not have to put on a brave front for other people.
